mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding demand-side momentum, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate revenue outlooks over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST growth trajectories (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real interest rates that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of periods with PAYEMS growth above 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 108.5 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate earnings expectations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 108.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and residential demand, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate revenue visibility over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined RSAFS and HOUST growth (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates for equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%.
